title: HTTP/1.1 Response Serialization, Field Order, Framing, and Writes · Runtime Contract: Field Output Injection
summary: A writer emits content before headers, injects unchecked field values, chooses conflicting framing, assumes one Write completes, and cannot prove exact transmitted bytes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the field output injection increment in canonical bounded HTTP/1.1 response serializer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.
Commit one status line, validated field section, empty line, framed content, and trailers in protocol order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Field Output Injection · Theory Field Output Injection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 7662 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to select one output framing mode while retaining partial-write and field-validation evidence; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Reject CR, LF, NUL, controls, invalid names, and unsafe values before serializing any response field.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Field Output Injection contract for the supplied data.
